summary.narrative: Mayor Bibb has delivered on several key campaign promises including transferring West Side Market operations to a nonprofit and creating a downtown tax-increment finance district. His administration has made progress on homelessness and crime initiatives, though police staffing remains a persistent challenge and some neighborhood quality-of-life promises have stalled.

promises.text: Turn over the West Side Market to a nonprofit operator
promises.evidence: The Bibb administration successfully transferred management of the historic West Side Market to a nonprofit operator, fulfilling a key campaign promise to revitalize the landmark.

promises.text: Create a tax-increment finance district in Downtown Cleveland
promises.evidence: In March 2024, City Council passed Mayor Bibb's legislation creating a tax-increment finance district in Downtown Cleveland as part of his 'core to shore' revitalization approach.
The administration projected reaching 'equilibrium' or an end to unsheltered homelessness within the first quarter of 2025, with significant progress made through coordinated housing initiatives.

promises.text: Increase enforcement targeted at violent crime hotspots
promises.evidence: Bibb implemented an 'all-of-government' approach to crime with increased enforcement targeted at the city's violent crime hotspots as promised.
The administration continues to face criticism from City Council over police staffing levels, with recruitment and retention remaining a persistent challenge.

promises.text: Revitalize thousands of vacant properties through neighborhood investment
promises.evidence: The administration has initiated programs to address vacant properties, but neighborhood quality-of-life concerns remain a point of City Council criticism.

promises.text: Implement 'core to shore' lakefront development strategy
promises.evidence: The core to shore approach has been implemented through multiple initiatives including the downtown TIF district, connecting Cleveland's downtown to its lakefront.

promises.text: Improve neighborhood quality of life citywide
promises.evidence: Despite stated commitments, the administration has faced ongoing criticism from City Council specifically regarding neighborhood quality-of-life issues that remain unaddressed.
